title: Games Hub // Game Reviews
summary: A public-safe reference on how serious game reviews, previews, scores, and review processes actually work.

landing.title: Build A Game Reviewer
landing.summary: How to think like a reviewer, not just a tester, when judging whether a game is worth a player's time and money.

sections.title: How To Write A Game Review
sections.summary: Structure, criticism, disclosure, and the difference between a useful review and a vague opinion dump.
sections.nextReviewTrigger: When the studio changes its reviewer workflow or public demo bar.

promptCards.title: Write a critic-style review
promptCards.body: Ask an agent to write a review that reads like criticism rather than QA notes.
promptCards.prompt: Act as a games critic. Based on this build, write a review with a clear thesis, context for what the game is trying to do, evidence-backed strengths, evidence-backed weaknesses, technical notes, and a closing verdict about whether it is worth a player's time right now.

sections.title: How To Score A Game
sections.summary: How major outlets use scales, when not to score at all, and how to avoid fake precision.
sections.nextReviewTrigger: When the studio changes its internal scoring scale or review modes.

promptCards.title: Score this build honestly
promptCards.body: Ask an agent to decide whether the build deserves a score or should stay unscored.
promptCards.prompt: Act as a senior games reviewer. Decide whether this build should be treated as a preview, review in progress, updated review, or final scored review. If it deserves a score, use a whole-number score out of 10 and explain exactly why. If it does not deserve a score, say what evidence is still missing.

sections.title: Review Process And Disclosure
sections.summary: Assignment, play time, disclosure, review-in-progress logic, and updated-review rules.
sections.nextReviewTrigger: When the studio changes reviewer cadence or disclosure policy.

promptCards.title: Set up a fair review pass
promptCards.body: Ask an agent to define the right review mode and disclosures before scoring a build.
promptCards.prompt: Act as a reviews editor. For this build, define the correct review mode, required disclosures, minimum play coverage, what must still be tested before scoring, and whether the output should be a preview, review in progress, updated review, or final review.

sections.title: Previews Vs Reviews
sections.summary: How to handle limited-access builds honestly and why previews should inform without pretending they are verdicts.
sections.nextReviewTrigger: When the Games news or reviewer workflow changes.

promptCards.title: Write a preview, not a review
promptCards.body: Ask an agent to stay honest when access is partial or the build is clearly unfinished.
promptCards.prompt: Act as a games journalist writing a hands-on preview. Describe what the build currently proves, what still cannot be judged, the biggest promise, the biggest concern, and why this should not yet be treated as a final review.

sections.title: Simulate Studio Feedback With A Reviewer
sections.summary: How small teams can use a standing reviewer session to create useful pressure, track progress, and feed criticism back into development.
sections.nextReviewTrigger: When the studio changes how often it runs review passes or how those reports feed back into production.

promptCards.title: Run a studio review pass
promptCards.body: Ask an agent to behave like a critic, not a developer defending the build.
promptCards.prompt: Act as an independent games reviewer. Review this build as if it were being evaluated for a public audience. Return the review mode, score if appropriate, verdict summary, strengths, weaknesses, technical concerns, target audience, and the single highest-leverage improvement before the next review pass.
